nameMeanings.name: Perry
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Welsh: Anglicized form of Welsh ap Herry ‘son of Herry’, a variant of the personal name Harry (see Harris ).

English: topographic name for someone who lived near a pear tree (Middle English piri(e), peri(e), puri(e), Old English pirige, pyrige), or a habitational name for someone who owned or came from a place so named. This surname and a number of variants have been established in Ireland since the 17th century.

variants.name: Peery
meanings: Irish: variant of   Perry  , taken to Ireland at the time of thePlantation of Ulster and in the Cromwellian period.

variants.name: Pear
meanings: English: topographic name from residence by a pear tree (Middle English pere, Old English peru, pere). Compare Perry .
English: nickname from Middle English pere, Old French per, peer ‘peer or paragon’, also ‘companion’.

variants.name: Peary
meanings: History: Robert E. Peary (1856–1920) was a U.S.-born explorer, who in 1909 led the first successful expedition to the North Pole. Peary Land in the north of Greenland is named after him, as he explored there in 1892 and 1900.

variants.name: Perrie
meanings: Scottish: variant of Perry 2. Alternatively, perhaps from the Old French personal name Pierre; see Pierce .

variants.name: Harris
meanings: English (southern England and south Wales): from the personal name Harry + genitival -s. This surname is also established in Ireland, taken there principally during the Plantation of Ulster. However, in some cases, particularly in families coming from County Mayo, Harris can be an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hEarchadha. This surname is also very common among African Americans.

American shortened and altered form of Greek surnames begining with Cha(r)-, such as Chasandrinos (variant of Kassandrinos, a habitational name from the Kassandra peninsula of Chalkidiki), and various patronymics from the personal name Charalampos (see Charos ). In North America, the surname Harris may possibly also originate from a transferred use of the Greek personal (given) name Charis or Harris (shortened forms of Charalampos) as a surname (i.e. as a replacement of the original surname).
Americanized form of various like-sounding Jewish surnames.

variants.name: Perryman
meanings: English (Devon): elaborated form of Perry , with Middle English man, probably denoting one who lived near a pear tree; see Pearman .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

en.lifeSummary: Margaret Perry was born on 8 August 1540, in Crewkerne, Somerset, England. She married John Perry in 1562, in England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 2 daughters. She died in 1612, in Somerset, England, at the age of 72, and was buried in Somerset, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Margaret Perry est née le 8 août 1540, à Crewkerne, Somerset, Angleterre. Elle a épousé John Perry en 1562, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée en 1612, à Somerset, Angleterre, à l’âge de 72 ans, et a été enterrée à Somerset, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Margaret Perry wurde am 8. August 1540 in Crewkerne, Somerset, England geboren. Sie hat 1562 in England, United Kingdom John Perry geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb 1612 im Alter von 72 Jahren in Somerset, England und wurde in Somerset, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Margaret Perry è nata il il 8 agosto 1540 nel Crewkerne, Somerset, England. Lei sposato John Perry nel 1562, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 2 figlie. Morì nel 1612, nel Somerset, England, all'età di 72 anni, e fu sepolta a Somerset, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret Perryは1540年8月8日にイギリスソメルセトクレウケルンで生まれた。1562年にイギリスイギリスでJohn Perryと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1612年にイギリスソメルセトで72歳で死去し，イギリスソメルセトにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Perry(은)는 1540년 8월 8일 영국 서머세트 크레w커니에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1562년 영국 잉글랜드에서 John Perry(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1612년 영국 서머세트에서 72세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 서머세트에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Margaret Perry nasceu em 8 de agosto de 1540, em Crewkerne, Somerset, Inglaterra. Ela casou-se com John Perry em 1562, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1612, em Somerset, Inglaterra, com 72 anos, e foi sepultada em Somerset, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Margaret Perry родилась 8 августа 1540 г. в местности крюкирн, сомирсет, Англия. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Perry в 1562 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 2 дочери. Она умерла в 1612 г. в местности сомирсет, Англия в возрасте 72 лет и была похоронена в местности сомирсет, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Margaret Perry nació el 8 de agosto de 1540, en Crewkerne, Somerset, Inglaterra. Se casó con John Perry en 1562, en Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió en 1612, en Somerset, Inglaterra, a la edad de 72 años, y fue sepultada en Somerset, Inglaterra.
